questions.text: Why is the story of Yusuf (AS) called the 'Best of Stories'?
questions.answer: Because of its detailed narrative, emotional depth, lessons on patience, and the demonstration of how Allah's plan prevails over human plotting.

questions.text: What was the dream Yusuf (AS) saw as a child?
questions.answer: He saw eleven stars, the sun, and the moon prostrating to him.

questions.text: What advice did Ya'qub (AS) give Yusuf after hearing his dream?
questions.answer: He told him not to relate his vision to his brothers, lest they plot against him out of jealousy.

questions.text: How did the brothers of Yusuf justify their plot against him?
questions.answer: They claimed that Yusuf and his brother were more beloved to their father than they were, though they were a strong group.

questions.text: What did the brothers decide to do with Yusuf instead of killing him?
questions.answer: They decided to throw him into the bottom of a well so that some travelers might pick him up.

questions.text: What evidence did the brothers bring to their father to prove Yusuf's death?
questions.answer: They brought his shirt stained with false blood, claiming a wolf had eaten him.

questions.text: What was the reaction of Ya'qub (AS) to the news of Yusuf's death?
questions.answer: He said, 'Patience is most fitting' (Sabrun Jameel) and suspected their story was fabricated.

questions.text: How much was Yusuf (AS) sold for by the travelers who found him?
questions.answer: For a small price—a few dirhams—because they considered him of little value.

questions.text: Who bought Yusuf (AS) after he was rescued from the well and taken to Egypt?
questions.answer: The Aziz (high official) of Egypt.

questions.text: What did the Aziz say to his wife when he brought Yusuf home?
questions.answer: He told her to make his stay honorable, as he might be of use to them or they might adopt him as a son.

questions.text: How did Yusuf (AS) respond to the temptation of the Aziz's wife?
questions.answer: He sought refuge in Allah, remaining loyal to his master and his faith, saying, 'Indeed, he is my master, who has made my stay honorable.'

questions.text: What 'witness' suggested the method to prove Yusuf's innocence?
questions.answer: A witness from her family noted that if his shirt was torn from the front, she was truthful; but if it was torn from the back, she was lying.

questions.text: Why did the women of the city cut their hands when they saw Yusuf?
questions.answer: They were so amazed by his extraordinary beauty that they said, 'This is not a man; this is none but a noble angel!'

questions.text: Why did Yusuf (AS) choose prison over the alternative?
questions.answer: He preferred prison over committing the sin the women were calling him toward, praying to Allah to avert their plot from him.

questions.text: Who entered prison along with Yusuf (AS)?
questions.answer: Two young men, who were servants of the King.

questions.text: What did Yusuf (AS) do first when the two prisoners asked him to interpret their dreams?
questions.answer: He invited them to the worship of Allah alone, emphasizing that sovereignty belongs only to Allah.

questions.text: What was the interpretation of the dream of the prisoner who saw himself pressing grapes?
questions.answer: He was told that he would be released and would again serve wine to his master.

questions.text: What was the interpretation for the prisoner who saw birds eating bread from his head?
questions.answer: He was told that he would be executed (crucified) and birds would eat from his head.

questions.text: What favor did Yusuf (AS) ask of the prisoner who was to be released?
questions.answer: To mention him to his master; however, Shaitan made the man forget, and Yusuf remained in prison for several more years.

questions.text: What was the King's dream that his counselors called 'confused dreams'?
questions.answer: Seven fat cows being eaten by seven lean ones, and seven green ears of grain and others dry.

questions.text: What was Yusuf's interpretation of the King's dream?
questions.answer: Seven years of abundance followed by seven years of severe famine, and then a year of rain and relief.

questions.text: What advice did Yusuf give for managing the years of abundance?
questions.answer: To leave the grain in its ears except for what is needed for food, to preserve it for the coming famine.

questions.text: Why did Yusuf (AS) refuse to leave prison when the King's messenger arrived?
questions.answer: He wanted his name cleared and the truth about the women's incident to be officially acknowledged first.

questions.text: What was the final confession of the Aziz's wife regarding Yusuf?
questions.answer: She said, 'Now the truth has become evident. It was I who sought to seduce him, and indeed, he is of the truthful.'

questions.text: What position did Yusuf (AS) ask for after being cleared and meeting the King?
questions.answer: To be in charge of the storehouses of the land (treasurer), as he was a 'knowing guardian'.

questions.text: How did Yusuf (AS) treat his brothers when they first came to Egypt for grain, even though they didn't recognize him?
questions.answer: He provided them with their supplies and was hospitable to them.

questions.text: What condition did Yusuf (AS) set for the brothers to receive grain again?
questions.answer: They must bring their youngest brother (Benjamin) with them next time.

questions.text: How did Yusuf (AS) ensure his brothers would have an incentive to return to Egypt?
questions.answer: He secretly returned their goods/money in their bags so they would find it when they got home.

questions.text: What was Ya'qub's condition for allowing Benjamin to go to Egypt with his brothers?
questions.answer: He made them take a solemn oath in the name of Allah to bring him back, unless they were completely overwhelmed.

questions.text: What advice did Ya'qub (AS) give his sons regarding how to enter the city of Egypt?
questions.answer: He told them not to enter by one gate but by different gates (to avoid the evil eye or suspicion).

questions.text: What did Yusuf (AS) secretly tell Benjamin when they were alone?
questions.answer: He said, 'Indeed, I am your brother, so do not despair over what they used to do.'

questions.text: What plan did Yusuf (AS) use to keep Benjamin with him in Egypt?
questions.answer: He placed the King's drinking cup in Benjamin's bag and then had it 'discovered' so he could be detained.

questions.text: What was the punishment for theft according to the brothers' own law?
questions.answer: The one in whose bag the stolen item was found would become a slave to the owner of the item.

questions.text: What did the brothers say about Yusuf when the cup was found with Benjamin?
questions.answer: They said if he stole, a brother of his (Yusuf) had also stolen before.

questions.text: Which brother refused to leave Egypt and return to his father without Benjamin?
questions.answer: The eldest brother, because of the oath he had given to his father.

questions.text: How did Ya'qub (AS) react to the news that Benjamin was detained for theft?
questions.answer: He said, 'Patience is most fitting' and prayed that Allah might bring all his sons back to him.

questions.text: What happened to Ya'qub's eyes due to his intense grief?
questions.answer: His eyes turned white (he became blind) because of the sorrow he was suppressing.

questions.text: What did Ya'qub (AS) say to his sons when they told him to stop remembering Yusuf?
questions.answer: He said, 'I only complain of my suffering and my grief to Allah, and I know from Allah that which you do not know.'

questions.text: What was the question Yusuf (AS) asked his brothers before revealing his identity to them?
questions.answer: He asked, 'Do you know what you did with Yusuf and his brother when you were ignorant?'

questions.text: What was the first thing Yusuf (AS) said to his brothers after they recognized him?
questions.answer: He told them there is no blame upon them that day and asked Allah to forgive them.

questions.text: How was Ya'qub's (AS) eyesight restored?
questions.answer: Yusuf sent his shirt to be cast over his father's face, and his vision returned.

questions.text: What did the father (Ya'qub) smell even before the caravan from Egypt reached him?
questions.answer: He said, 'Indeed, I find the smell of Yusuf,' though those around him thought he was deluded.

questions.text: What did the brothers ask of their father after they returned from Egypt with the news?
questions.answer: They asked him to seek forgiveness for their sins, admiting they had been wrongdoers.

questions.text: What did Yusuf (AS) do for his parents when they arrived in Egypt?
questions.answer: He welcomed them, raised them on the throne, and they all fell down in prostration to him.

questions.text: What was Yusuf's reflection on his childhood dream at the end of the story?
questions.answer: He said, 'O my father, this is the explanation of my vision of before. My Lord has made it a reality.'

questions.text: What was Yusuf's final prayer (Dua) mentioned in this Surah?
questions.answer: He thanked Allah for sovereignty and knowledge and asked to die as a Muslim and be joined with the righteous.

questions.text: Does Allah inform the Prophet Muhammad about the unseen through such stories?
questions.answer: Yes, 'That is from the news of the unseen which We reveal to you, [O Muhammad].'

questions.text: What is the core lesson of the Surah regarding the 'Command of Allah'?
questions.answer: Allah is predominant over His affairs, but most of the people do not know.

questions.text: How does the Surah describe the 'soul' (Nafs) in relation to evil?
questions.answer: The soul is an enjoyner of evil, except those upon whom my Lord has mercy.

questions.text: What is the reward for those who have 'Taqwa' (God-consciousness) and are patient?
questions.answer: Allah does not allow the reward of those who do good to be lost.

questions.text: What is the 'clear proof' mentioned in the introduction of the Surah?
questions.answer: The Quran itself, revealed in Arabic so that people may understand.

questions.text: Why did Yusuf's brothers think they were more deserving of their father's love?
questions.answer: Because they were a 'strong group' (Usbah), whereas Yusuf and Benjamin were just two young children.

questions.text: What was the 'blood' on Yusuf's shirt?
questions.answer: It was the blood of a goat or sheep that the brothers had slaughtered to deceive their father.

questions.text: What is the 'Sabrun Jameel' mentioned by Ya'qub (AS)?
questions.answer: A 'beautiful patience' that is without complaining to people, only to Allah.

questions.text: What did the person in the caravan say when he found Yusuf in the well?
questions.answer: He shouted, 'O good news! Here is a boy!'

questions.text: How did Allah prepare Yusuf for his future role while he was in Egypt?
questions.answer: He established him in the land and taught him the interpretation of events/dreams.

questions.text: What was the 'evidence' from Allah that saved Yusuf from temptation?
questions.answer: A spiritual sign or insight that made him realize the gravity of the sin.

questions.text: How did the Aziz react when he saw the shirt was torn from the back?
questions.answer: He said, 'Indeed, it is from the plot of you women. Indeed, your plot is great.'

questions.text: What did the Aziz's wife say she would do to Yusuf if he continued to refuse her?
questions.answer: She threatened to have him imprisoned and made of those who are debased.

questions.text: What was Yusuf's Dawah regarding 'separate lords' versus 'Allah'?
questions.answer: He asked, 'Are separate lords better or Allah, the One, the Prevailing?'

questions.text: What did Yusuf (AS) say about the names the polytheists worshipped?
questions.answer: He said they are nothing but names they and their fathers had invented, for which Allah had sent down no authority.

questions.text: What was the 'year of relief' Yusuf predicted?
questions.answer: A year when people would have rain and would press [grapes and olives].

questions.text: How did the King describe Yusuf after meeting him?
questions.answer: He said, 'Indeed, you are today established in our presence and trusted.'

questions.text: What was the 'mercy' that Allah gave to Yusuf in the end?
questions.answer: He settled him in the land to dwell therein wherever he willed.

questions.text: What did the brothers say when they entered Yusuf's presence without knowing him?
questions.answer: They spoke of their hardship and asked for grain.

questions.text: What did the brothers tell Benjamin's father regarding their previous loss?
questions.answer: They reminded him of how they had lost Yusuf, but promised to protect Benjamin.

questions.text: What was the 'burden of a camel' mentioned in the context of the grain?
questions.answer: The amount of grain that a camel could carry, which was the measure given to each person.

questions.text: How did the brothers feel when they found their money back in their bags?
questions.answer: They were happy and used it as an argument to convince their father to let Benjamin go.

questions.text: What did Yusuf (AS) do with Benjamin in the palace?
questions.answer: He kept him close and comforted him.

questions.text: What was the 'King's cup' made of?
questions.answer: The Surah implies it was a precious vessel used for drinking and measuring.

questions.text: What did the announcer cry out when the cup was missing?
questions.answer: 'O caravan, indeed you are thieves!'

questions.text: Why could Yusuf not have taken his brother under the King's law?
questions.answer: Because the King's law had different punishments for theft; it was Allah's plan to use the brothers' own law.

questions.text: What is 'above every possessor of knowledge'?
questions.answer: 'Above every possessor of knowledge is one more knowing' (referring to Allah).

questions.text: What did the eldest brother tell his father to do to verify their story?
questions.answer: To ask the people of the city and the caravan they had traveled with.

questions.text: What did Ya'qub (AS) say about 'despairing of the mercy of Allah'?
questions.answer: He said, 'Indeed, no one despairs of the relief of Allah except the disbelieving people.'

questions.text: What did the brothers bring as 'meager goods' in their final visit?
questions.answer: They had very little to offer in exchange for grain and asked for charity.

questions.text: What did the brothers say when they realized Yusuf's identity?
questions.answer: 'Are you indeed Yusuf?' and he replied, 'I am Yusuf, and this is my brother.'

questions.text: What did Yusuf (AS) tell his family to do after he was revealed?
questions.answer: To bring all their family members to Egypt to live with him.

questions.text: What is the 'lesson' for people of understanding mentioned in the the closing ayah?
questions.answer: That the stories of the prophets are not fabricated but are a confirmation of truth and a guidance.

questions.text: What is the final status of the 'believing' people at the end of the Surah?
questions.answer: They are the ones who will have success and the best home in the Hereafter.
